item.title: My friend just got backed into by a cop. The cop is denying they made contact.
item.description:
Here you can see the cops bumper on the right, and the scrape on my friends fender on the left.
Here is the only mud on the cop's car, but the cop claims it is just a coincidence.
What do y'all think? I'm calling shenanigans!

item.dc:creator: samme12345678920

item.description: I'm mentally handicapped, high-functioning enough to know it, but not enough to do much more than mask. I'm never going to own a home, I'm unlikely to go back into full-time work for any reasonable stretch of time, and I'm honestly unlikely to ever be self-supporting. The only thing separating me from the unhoused is that my parents were there to be my safety net, and I don't want to be a pensioner of their kindness for the rest of my, or their, lives. I'm medicated, and happy enough most days, but I don't see happiness in my future. I really want the option to just go on my own terms. I don't think I'll be doing it any time soon, but it's what I really want; not a whim, not a desparate last ditch, but something I've thought long and hard about. I'm not looking to fill a GoFundWhatever, or be talked out of this. I just really needed to put it up somewhere. Those of you who stopped to read, thank you. Please, talk to the people around you. Let them know you love them. -K
item.dc:creator: NottinghamBlue

item.title: Missed my chance
item.description: Hey random internet person. You made a post here today. You were looking for a reason to live. I opened it, tried to figure out what to say in the comments, got distracted and closed it. That was several hours ago, there's no way I'm gonna track down a solitary post on here, but I thought of what I needed to say. I'll be the reason. I'm just a random guy, we've never met and almost certainly never will. But if I can be a reason for someone to get up tomorrow and give life another go, then that's what I'm gonna do. Just an offer, no pressure to take me up on it. And it's just as well if you do it for a cat or a flower or a song. See ya tomorrow?
item.dc:creator: We11thatwasC00L

item.title: Images of Note: Smug Yoda
item.description:
It's me, I'm the weird aunt
It's also bad luck to say MacOS, you have to call it "closed source BSD".
http://twitch.tv/abbiistabbii I have gotten the Mayonnaise maker for this one reason.
It's not Taylor Swift's execution, it's execution by Taylor Swift.
He looks like he just watched Ireland get thrashed by Italy in Rugby (and to be fair, I would look the same way if that happened to Scotland).
it's because it's noisy, bright and stressful.
D:
But he was bald in Ragnarok, what does that mean? WHAT DOES THAT MEAN?
I have....come.
I would like Emily better if she looked like that.
THIS IS AN ACTUAL STORY AND IT'S FUCKING WILD! https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gimli_Glider
To be fair, neither does Liz Truss.
WE'RE FUCKIN' COMIN'
For Chats: http://discord.abbii.gay For Shitposts: http://tumblr.abbii.gay For srs and maybe shitposts: http://mastodon.abbii.gay Also I'm streamin': http://twitch.abbii.gay
item.dc:creator: abbiistabbii

item.title: Have you ever been so happy?
item.dc:creator: vladrichdemaclant

item.title: ACT UP was an extremely significant part of history. I’d love to hear your part in it, if you don’t mind.
item.description:
EDIT: “A PART” NOT “APART!” MY BAD!!! . Two months ago, I saw a scene from Straight Outta Compton, where Eazy-E is diagnosed with AIDS. It greatly affected me, and I decided to work on a post series about it. . Part 1: The science of HIV/AIDS and health safety: https://imgur.com/gallery/qFnjuLe Part 2: The historical, social, and political impacts this virus had on the world (still working on it) . I’ve been working on this since January 16th. Unlike my other posts on history, I have a chance to interview primary sources, and scour every documentary and news report on it. In fact, I almost gave up on this post because there were so many pieces to it. It was stressful because leaving out a detail is leaving out a group of people who were destroyed by the crisis. Fortunately, I’ve found a way to make this post work. It’s going to take me another week, and I might take a week or two for a mental health break, but I’m DETERMINED to make this post. I’ve interviewed my parents and their friends, but none of them were a part of the ACT UP protests. These protests were a significant part of history, especially how they saved so many lives by twisting the FDA’s arm into approving HIV medication, and making it more affordable to people. I’d like to learn more about your part in this history, and include it in my post in some fashion. Thanks so much for your help! :) . . EDIT 2: Can we please stop messaging and commenting about me using the wrong word? I’m close to taking this one down and reposting it, but it hit the front page, so I want to wait on that. Listen, I understand that I made a careless mistake. I’m frustrated that we’re hung up on this, because it detracts from the post. I’d also like you to understand that I have spent weeks reading things like: "20 month old infant died of AIDS" (repeat that 50 times over) “There were bumper stickers that said 'AIDS: It's Killing All The Right People!" I’ve been watching documentaries where almost every subject died soon after being interviewed. I’ve been reading patches from the AIDS quilt that spans longer than a shopping mall. I’m worn down, okay? I made a careless mistake, and I might end up making a couple of more. PLEASE, PLEASE let this post stick to the goal of finding people who were in this protest so I can get their story!
Also, if you lost someone to AIDS, I want to say sorry for your loss. If you'd like to send me a message with a memory of them, I'll make an extra post that features all the memories Imgurians send me.
item.dc:creator: OlivetheNerd

How it started: Oscar
How it's going: Oscar "I Are Sweetest Boy"
How it started: Velma
How it's going: Velma "It's 4:55pm. Log Out NOW"
How it started: Daphne
How it's going: Daphne "Resting Bitch Face"
How it started: Gertie
How it's going: Gertie "Peripheral Cord Killer"
Workplace meeting.
